summary: The dark-mode implementation is well-structured: semantic CSS tokens repaint under a class-based .dark variant, a pre-paint inline head script prevents FOUC, the server stamps the dark class from the cookie, and there are no security concerns (no active CSP, static non-interpolated inline script restricted to dark|light, non-sensitive SameSite=Lax cookie). View conversion to semantic utilities is complete and the feature is covered by helper + system tests. One correctness bug: the OS-preference change listener in the Stimulus controller is not removed after the user makes an explicit choice, so a mid-session OS theme change can silently override the user's manual selection.

findings.issue: connect() attaches a matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)') 'change' listener whenever no theme cookie exists yet. toggle() then writes the cookie (establishing an explicit preference) but never removes that listener. Within the same page session (no Turbo/full navigation to trigger disconnect+reconnect), if the OS theme subsequently changes, onSystemChange fires and unconditionally sets the class to media.matches, overriding the user's explicit toggle choice and contradicting the 'manual choice wins over system default' behavior. Fix: remove the listener in toggle() once a preference is stored (or have onSystemChange early-return when hasStoredPreference() is true).
